name: Jainam Shah — Senior QA Automation Engineer
subject: Jainam — when Amount ships, how many tests break?
body:
Hi Jainam,

Building automated tests for a banking-as-a-service platform usually means your test base is constantly patching broken locators when APIs shift. Is that something eating into your team's sprint time?

Medibuddy was dealing with the same maintenance drag and cut it 50% on Testsigma. Their test engineering team stopped spending sprints on locator repair and started writing new coverage.

At Amount's scale, regression stability compounds across every release cycle. How much of your current sprint capacity is going to test maintenance versus building new coverage?

Cheers,
Rob

name: Ilija Andic — QA Automation Manager
subject: Ilija — which platform breaks Incode tests first?
body:
Hi Ilija,

Testing a biometric identity platform means covering mobile, web, and API surfaces simultaneously — and keeping regressions stable across all three tends to be where test teams hit complexity walls. How is your team managing that coordination?

Samsung was dealing with the same multi-platform challenge and got consistent regression coverage across iOS, Android, and web using Testsigma. No longer debugging different failures across platforms.

At Incode's growth pace, fragmented test coverage creates risk. How are you currently managing regression consistency across your different platforms?

Cheers,
Rob

name: Jyoti Jain — Principal QA Engineer/QA Team Lead
subject: Jyoti — keeping coverage across WorkWave's 4 verticals?
body:
Hi Jyoti,

With WorkWave's platform supporting field service, pest control, landscaping, and security workflows, test surface area probably grows faster than team headcount can cover. Is that something your team is actively trying to get ahead of?

CRED hit 90% regression coverage and 5x faster execution with Testsigma. Their team stopped worrying about coverage gaps and started shipping velocity improvements instead.

At WorkWave's scale, that shift compounds across every release. How much of your critical regression scope does your current suite cover today?

Cheers,
Rob

name: Jeffrey Miller — IT Quality Assurance and DevOps Manager
subject: Jeffrey — how much of EverBank's sprint is fixing tests?
body:
Hi Jeffrey,

Managing QA at a digital bank usually means your team spends more time patching broken tests than building new ones. Is keeping regression tests stable from sprint to sprint something your team is actively wrestling with?

Hansard was dealing with the same brittleness problem and cut their regression cycle from 8 weeks to 5 weeks with Testsigma, without adding engineers. Their team stopped manually fixing locators every release.

At EverBank's release cadence, that maintenance compounds fast. How often are false failures from broken locators showing up in your runs right now?

Cheers,
Rob
